atoms.semantic_claim: The old downstream EVM crate added direct revm context and precompile dependencies so async-context code could compile against revm context/precompile APIs.

destinations.evidence: The renamed reth-evm manifest no longer depends directly on revm-context or revm-precompile; it depends on revm and maps async-context to revm/async-context.
revm async-context fans out to context/async-context, handler/async-context, and precompile/async-context.
revm-context retains an async-context feature with tracing and spin dependencies.
revm-precompile retains async-context = ["context", "context/async-context", "tracing"].

atoms.equivalence_argument: The old direct dependency surface is not present in the destination reth-evm crate, but the behavior-bearing feature edge moved behind the revm crate's async-context aggregate, which reaches the same context and precompile crates.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: Disable revm/async-context in reth-evm and confirm async precompile/context code no longer compiles or dispatches.
atoms.notes: Reviewed: covered by the v2 feature/dependency split. This is support/manifest surface only, with no FSM/runtime gap.

atoms.semantic_claim: The old downstream EVM crate added once_cell as async-context support utility surface.

destinations.evidence: The v2 reth-evm manifest does not retain once_cell as a direct dependency.
The nodebuilder precompile crate carries once_cell alongside async-context precompile dependencies.
once_cell remains available in the revm workspace where primitive-level static helpers live.

atoms.equivalence_argument: The utility dependency is no longer needed by the split reth-evm crate itself; remaining uses live in downstream precompile and revm support crates that own the corresponding static/helper behavior.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: Remove once_cell from ritual-precompiles or revm primitives and confirm any static async precompile helper usage fails at compile time.
atoms.notes: No direct once_cell edge remains in v2 reth-evm.

atoms.semantic_claim: The old downstream EVM crate added txpool, metrics, and TEE registry reader dependencies for async execution and verification plumbing.

destinations.evidence: The payload builder owns reth-transaction-pool and ritual-tee-registry-reader dependencies for async payload construction.
Block verification owns ritual-tee-registry-reader and other async verification dependencies.
Nodebuilder ritual-node depends on ritual-metrics, ritual-tee-registry-reader, reth-transaction-pool, reth-ethereum-payload-builder, and default async-context precompile wiring.
The nodebuilder workspace maps ritual-metrics and ritual-tee-registry-reader as workspace dependencies, while reth-transaction-pool points at the v2.2 port.

atoms.equivalence_argument: The v2 architecture moves txpool and registry behavior out of the core reth-evm manifest and into payload, block-verification, and nodebuilder orchestration crates. The dependency intent is preserved as orchestration surface rather than direct EVM crate surface.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: Remove ritual-tee-registry-reader from payload/block-verification or remove reth-transaction-pool from nodebuilder/payload and confirm async payload or verifier wiring fails to compile.
atoms.notes: Reviewed: covered by the v2 feature/dependency split. This is support/manifest surface only, with no FSM/runtime gap.

atoms.semantic_claim: The old downstream EVM crate added tracing and tokio runtime support for async-context code paths.

destinations.evidence: Payload builder retains tracing while async runtime ownership is elsewhere in the node stack.
The transaction pool owns tokio sync/runtime features and tracing.
revm-context async-context pulls tracing and spin, not tokio, for context-level async support.
Nodebuilder precompiles carry tracing and tokio for precompile-side async behavior.

atoms.equivalence_argument: The destination removes tokio/tracing from core reth-evm and places them in runtime-owning payload, txpool, revm context, and nodebuilder precompile crates. That preserves build surface where async work is actually orchestrated.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: Remove tokio from txpool or nodebuilder precompiles and confirm runtime-bound async code no longer compiles.
atoms.notes: The v2 reth-evm crate has no direct tokio or tracing dependency.

atoms.semantic_claim: The old downstream EVM crate made async-context part of the default feature set.

destinations.evidence: The renamed reth-evm manifest has default = ["std", "async-context"].
alloy-evm also defaults to ["std", "async-context"], matching the default-on downstream async feature model.
ritual-node defaults to async-context.

atoms.equivalence_argument: The exact default feature atom is preserved in the destination reth-evm manifest after the v2 crate path rename.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: Change reth-evm default back to ["std"] and confirm async-context-dependent downstream default builds lose the feature.
atoms.notes: This is the strongest exact mapping in this file.

atoms.semantic_claim: The old downstream async-context feature explicitly enabled Alloy EVM async support plus revm precompile/context async support.

destinations.evidence: reth-evm async-context fans out to alloy-evm/async-context and revm/async-context.
alloy-evm async-context enables optional revm-context and revm/async-context.
revm/async-context fans out to context, handler, and precompile async-context features.
ritual-precompiles directly enables revm-context, revm-precompile, and revm-handler async-context.

atoms.equivalence_argument: The old explicit revm-context and revm-precompile feature edges are represented by the new aggregate revm/async-context edge and reinforced by nodebuilder precompile feature wiring. The semantic fanout is preserved, but the feature graph is not textually identical.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: Remove revm/async-context from reth-evm async-context and confirm revm precompile/context async cfgs are absent from the feature graph.
atoms.notes: Reviewed: covered by the v2 feature/dependency split. This is support/manifest surface only, with no FSM/runtime gap.

atoms.semantic_claim: The old downstream async-context feature also pulled Ethereum primitive support and reth execution error async support into the EVM feature graph.

destinations.evidence: reth-evm async-context retains reth-execution-errors/async-context; reth-ethereum-primitives is present as a dev dependency and in the std/test-utils feature surface.
reth-evm-ethereum owns reth-ethereum-primitives as a normal dependency and exposes async-context = ["reth-evm/async-context"].
reth-ethereum async-context enables evm, reth-evm/async-context, and reth-evm-ethereum/async-context.
reth-execution-errors keeps async-context = ["alloy-evm/async-context"].

atoms.equivalence_argument: Execution-error async feature propagation remains directly represented, while Ethereum primitive ownership moved to the v2 ethereum-specific EVM crate and top-level reth-ethereum feature fanout. This matches the v2 crate split rather than the old monolithic EVM manifest shape.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: Remove reth-evm-ethereum/async-context from reth-ethereum async-context and confirm ethereum primitive async-context integration is lost from the top-level feature graph.
atoms.notes: Reviewed: covered by the v2 feature/dependency split. This is support/manifest surface only, with no FSM/runtime gap.

adversarial_reviews.finding: Most async-context edges are structurally mapped through v2 aggregate crates rather than exact textual dependencies; cargo feature-resolution evidence is the right validation target.
ritual-metrics is not in the v2.2 reth port workspace manifest and is currently evidenced through nodebuilder workspace paths, so reviewer attention is warranted.

summary: Mechanical review complete. The old EVM manifest async-context dependencies and features are covered by the v2 feature graph and ownership split: reth-evm forwards async-context to Alloy EVM, revm, and execution-errors, while txpool/registry/metrics runtime ownership moved to payload, block-verification, and nodebuilder crates. No FSM/runtime gap remains; this row is manifest/support only.
notes: Reviewed against latest port commit 7f8a668d24. Non-exact atoms are dependency relocation or feature-graph mappings, not missing behavior.
